passage.title: Weekend Chore Strip
passage.text: Our group keeps a dinner helper board on the kitchen wall. After school, Evan points to it before our family walk. The dinner helper board has small notes for watering, feeding, and folding for the day's job. My older sister asks us to match each card to the right person. Sometimes we compare today's note with yesterday's note and notice one small change. We like the dinner helper board because it helps us finish dinner work on time.

questions.prompt: What is this passage mostly about?
questions.rationale: The whole passage explains how the dinner helper board is used.

questions.prompt: Where is the dinner helper board?
questions.rationale: The first sentence says the dinner helper board is on the kitchen wall.

questions.prompt: When does Evan use the dinner helper board?
questions.rationale: The second sentence says Evan uses it after school.

questions.prompt: What does the dinner helper board have?
questions.rationale: The third sentence says the dinner helper board has job notes.

questions.prompt: What does compare mean in the fifth sentence?
questions.rationale: Compare means to look at two things and think about them together.

questions.prompt: What does notice mean in the fifth sentence?
questions.rationale: Notice means to see and pay attention to something.
